channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: RFK Jr. made promises to get his job as health secretary. He's broken many of them
item.description: In his confirmation hearings, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. told U.S. senators that he would not cut funding for vaccine research or change the nation's offic...

item.title: Colorectal cancer is leading cancer killer for people under 50
item.description: James Van Der Beek's death at age 48 highlights a sad statistic: More people under 50 are getting colorectal cancer.

item.title: A telephone-based intervention reduced loneliness and improved well-being among older adults
item.description: 1. Four weeks of telephone-delivered behavioral activation and mindfulness interventions by lay counsellors were associated with reduced loneliness an...

item.title: Judge blocks Trump admin from rescinding health grants to Democratic-led states
item.description: The ruling temporarily blocks the Trump Administration from cutting $600 million in public health grants that had already been allocated to four Democ...

item.title: Sex Disparity Seen in Response to Growth Therapy in 21-OHD
item.description: In patients with classical 21-hydroxylase deficiency, growth-promoting therapies have variable effects: They increase final height relative to midpare...

item.title: EU Withdrawal of Levamisole Recommended Due to CNS Risks
item.description: Europe’s medicines regulator confirmed that leukoencephalopathy is a rare but serious side effect of levamisole-containing medicines. Medscape News Eu...

item.title: Microfluidic chip finds viable eggs for IVF that doctors may have missed
item.description: Infertility affects approximately 8% to 12% of couples of reproductive age worldwide, and IVF (in vitro fertilization) is often the go-to treatment op...

item.title: Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes can lower dengue risk by 70%, citywide experiment finds
item.description: Dengue is a mosquito-borne virus affecting millions of people each year, with symptoms ranging from flu-like illness to severe bleeding and organ fail...

item.title: Frontal brain signal tied to compulsive behaviors in people with OCD
item.description: A specific pattern of brain activity in a frontal brain region is linked to compulsive behaviors like excessive hand washing, chronic hair-pulling, an...

item.title: Can tuberculosis treatment be safely shortened? New studies look inside the lungs for answers
item.description: Across the spectrum of human afflictions—from cancer to heart disease to rare genetic conditions—medical investigators are continually attempting to b...

item.title: Tobacco cessation offers untapped revenue for medical practices [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Board-certified internist with a long-standing focus on public health...

item.title: NHS 3.3% Pay Rise Condemned by Unions
item.description: The government says the 3.3% NHS pay award is above forecast inflation, but unions warn it will deepen recruitment and retention pressures. Medscape N...

item.title: Video Interpreters Improve Care for Deaf Patients
item.description: Presence of a live video interpreter during consultations may help improve some aspects of communication between doctors and patients with deafness. M...

item.title: Why medical students need health care economics
item.description: These days, it is not uncommon to hear complaints from doctors, nurses, and other clinical staff about policies made by “higher-ups.” Stories of physi...

item.title: Sex Hormones Linked to CV Risk in Men but Not Women With T2D
item.description: Findings from the LookAHEAD trial also show that the effect of sex hormones differed by the amount of weight loss. Medscape Medical News

item.title: From Williams-Sonoma to medicine: What retail taught me about difficult patients
item.description: Toward the end of undergrad, I was desperate for money to support myself and feed my figurative babies, which at the time took the form of MCAT prep a...

item.title: How psychedelic drugs affect the brain: Animal study links hallucinations to memory fragments
item.description: Psychedelic substances are increasingly being used under medical supervision to treat anxiety disorders and depression. However, the mechanisms by whi...

item.title: Olympic athletes push their bodies to the limit. Should we?
item.description: Elite athletes often push through pain to achieve victory. But, everyday exercisers need to distinguish between soreness which is normal and pain whic...

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: Court rebuffs plea from domestic workers for better pay and respect
item.description: They're often paid low wages and lack job protections. A petition to the country's supreme court to support their demands did not see success — and th...

item.title: Executive order on homelessness: Why forced treatment fails
item.description: In his recent executive order, Ending Crime and Disorder on America’s Streets, President Trump directed federal agencies to utilize forced institution...

item.title: The medical referral process: Why it fails and how to fix it
item.description: For clinicians, trainees, and health system leaders, the referral process is meant to connect patients to the specialty care they need. But in everyda...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
item.description: 1. In this randomized controlled trial, once-daily oral enlicitide was associated with sustained reductions in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol lev...
